input.background: The smoking prevalence example in this chapter $(n=57, x=17)$ estimated a population proportion of 0.30 with margin of error (assuming $95 \%$ confidence) equal to $\pm 0.12$.
input.question: How large a sample would be needed to shrink this margin of error to plus or minus 0.03?

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量

sample_size_n.description: The initial sample size used in the smoking prevalence example.

number_of_successes_x.description: The number of successes (e.g., smokers) in the initial sample.

estimated_population_proportion.description: The estimated proportion of the population that smokes.

initial_margin_of_error.description: The initial margin of error for the estimated population proportion with 95% confidence.

confidence_level.description: The confidence level used to calculate the margin of error.

desired_margin_of_error.description: The desired margin of error for the population proportion estimate.
